page: Amazon Web Services m8id.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a strong single-core performance profile but limited multi-core scaling. The instance achieved a strong Geekbench single-core score of 2162.0 and a strong single-threaded gzip compression rate of 33.96 GB/sec. However, multi-core metrics are weaker, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 2158.88 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 2695.0, both falling into lower performance percentiles. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with uncached large-block read speeds of 22.4 GB/sec and write speeds of 15.48 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8id.large offers a balanced general-purpose resource profile with high-speed DDR5 memory and integrated NVMe storage, reducing the need for separate block storage attachments for temporary data. However, the low vCPU count and weak multi-core scaling limit its utility for highly parallelized tasks. This instance is best suited for single-threaded applications, basic text processing, lightweight web serving, and development environments that benefit from fast local scratch space but do not require intensive multi-core computation or high network throughput.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8id.large is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It provides 2 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store. While single-core benchmarks are strong, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2162.0 and high single-threaded compression throughput, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak. With a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps, this instance offers a balanced hardware profile for workloads that require fast local storage and single-threaded execution, such as text processing, lightweight web serving, and development environments, though it is less suited for highly parallelized or memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD. It offers strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 Intel vCPUs, 8 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 118 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: A general-purpose instance combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing and fast local NVMe storage for single-threaded and scratch-space workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs at 3.9 GHz
8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz
Bundled 118 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Strong single-core and single-threaded compression performance
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and development
